scroll_found.title: Research the Living Decay scroll at a Research Pedestal

ancient_city_loot.title: Loot the deep chests in an Ancient City — retrieve the hidden items without waking more Wardens

subtitle.text: The Sculk Sickness has five stages. Stage 5 is not recoverable without the cure. The cure is in The End.

description: CH.08 · THE LIVING DECAY
▸ The Living Decay scroll describes Sculk Sickness
from the perspective of someone who survived Stage 4.
They barely survived Stage 4.
They describe what Stage 5 looks like from the outside.
They don't describe it from the inside.
No one has.
◈ SCULK SICKNESS — FULL REFERENCE
└ Stage 1: Mining Fatigue — annoying
└ Stage 2: Weakness added — now affecting combat
└ Stage 3: Slowness stack — movement impaired
└ Stage 4: Vision effects — dangerous in combat
└ Stage 5: Full debuff suite — nearly incapacitated
◈ CURE PATH
└ Ambrosia: crafted from specific End materials
(requires End dimension access — Ch13)
└ Vile Herb: partially mitigates progression
also used in Cursed Eye recipe (Ch12)
└ Prevention: don't mine Sculk, don't trigger Shriekers,
don't linger in Deep Dark biome
◈ ANCIENT CITY LOOT
└ Best pre-End loot in the game
└ Contains: Swift Sneak enchant books,
Echo Shards (for Compass of Recovery),
unique armor pieces, ISS spell scrolls
└ Strategy: Sneak everywhere. Never sprint.
Wool blocks muffle vibrations.
Enter at night or with Cat/Cloak potion.
◈ SCROLL: The Living Decay
└ Found in: Ancient City deep loot chests,
Warden chamber relics, sculk ruin deposits
└ Research time: 30 min
└ Unlocks: full Sculk Sickness stage guide,
Ambrosia cure path, Ancient City strategy
▶ Find The Living Decay scroll [check]
▶ Loot Ancient City deep chests without waking Wardens [check]
◇ Ambrosia (End materials) is the only cure for Stage 5.
If you hit Stage 5 before Ch13,
use Vile Herb repeatedly to slow progression.
Do not fight the Warden at Stage 4+.
